stories.title: Google ordered to put clearer links in AI search and let UK publishers opt out
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: Google faces regulatory pressure to improve transparency in its AI Overviews.
The ruling mandates clearer source linking within Google's AI-generated search results.
UK publishers are granted the option to opt out of being cited by Google's AI search features.

stories.title: Humanoid-GPT: Scaling Data and Structure for Zero-Shot Motion Tracking
stories.author: Li Yi
bullet_points: Introduction of Humanoid-GPT, a GPT-style Transformer designed for whole-body control in humanoid robotics.
The model is trained on a massive, unified corpus (2B frames) combining major mocap datasets and in-house recordings.
It addresses limitations of prior shallow trackers by scaling data and model capacity to achieve zero-shot tracking of highly dynamic behaviors.

stories.title: Gemma 4 12B: A unified, encoder-free multimodal model
stories.author: rvz
bullet_points: Introduction of 'Gemma 4 12B', a new large language model.
The model features a unified, encoder-free multimodal architecture.
The announcement originates from Hacker News, suggesting high technical interest.

stories.title: Full Disclosure: 1-Click GitHub Token Stealing via a VSCode Bug
stories.author: blog.ammaraskar.com by ammar2
bullet_points: A critical vulnerability exists in VSCode that allows for one-click GitHub token theft.
The exploit targets GitHub tokens, posing a severe security risk to developers and repositories.
The disclosure highlights the importance of rigorous security auditing in development tools.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance optimization.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, leveraging Apple's native ML framework.
The feature is currently available in a preview state, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It connects these apps to local AI coding agents.
A key feature is that all processing runs entirely on the user's local device, emphasizing privacy.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is partnering with OpenAI and ROOST to integrate gpt-oss-safeguard models.
These models are designed for safety classification tasks and are available in 20B and 120B sizes.
The gpt-oss-safeguard models are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license, promoting open use.

stories.title: Nvidia is already planning N2X and N3X chips — the goal is the Star Trek computer
stories.author: Sean Hollister
bullet_points: Nvidia is planning future chip generations, specifically N2X and N3X, beyond the initial RTX Spark.
CEO Jensen Huang confirmed at Computex 2026 that RTX Spark is not a one-off product.
The development suggests a strategic push by Nvidia into consumer laptop chip market segments.

stories.title: Amazon’s search bar will invent AI-generated products you can’t buy
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Amazon is integrating AI into its search bar to generate product images based on user descriptions.
Currently, the feature is limited to generating AI images for clothing and home goods.
Users can select a generated image to find and purchase similar, real-world items on Amazon.

stories.title: Google's new Gemma 4 12B model is designed to run on any laptop with 16GB of RAM
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google has released Gemma 4 12B, a new large language model.
The model is optimized for local deployment, capable of running on laptops with 16GB of RAM.
Performance gains are attributed to a new encoding scheme and token prediction methodology.

stories.title: Agentic Chain-of-Thought Steering for Efficient and Controllable LLM Reasoning
stories.author: Julian McAuley
bullet_points: Introduces Agentic Chain-of-Thought Steering (ACTS) to improve LLM reasoning efficiency and controllability.
Addresses the inefficiency and lack of inference-time control in standard extended chain-of-thought reasoning.
Models reasoning steering as a Markov decision process managed by a controller agent.

stories.title: AlphaQ: Calibration-Free Bit Allocation for Mixture-of-Experts Quantization
stories.author: Shiwei Liu
bullet_points: Introduces AlphaQ, a new method for bit allocation in Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models.
Addresses the memory bottleneck in MoE deployment by enabling mixed-precision quantization across experts.
Crucially, AlphaQ achieves this calibration-free, removing the dependency on extensive calibration data for bit allocation.

stories.title: Be Fair! Can Machine Learning Engineering Agents Adhere to Fairness Constraints?
stories.author: Sebastian Schelter
bullet_points: The paper addresses the challenge of ensuring fairness when using automated Machine Learning Engineering (MLE) agents.
MLE agents promise to democratize ML by automating pipelines from natural language instructions.
A key concern is the 'responsibility gap' where end-users lack visibility into design choices affecting fairness and compliance.

stories.title: STaR-Quant: State-Time Consistent Post-Training Quantization for Diffusion Large Language Models
stories.author: Xingrui Yuand Ivor Tsang
bullet_points: Addresses the computational overhead of Diffusion Large Language Models (DLLMs) through post-training quantization.
Identifies and tackles two specific quantization challenges in DLLMs: state-dependent activation disparity and related issues.
Proposes STaR-Quant, a method designed for State-Time Consistent Post-Training Quantization to improve deployment efficiency.

stories.title: Sequential Data Poisoning in LLM Post-Training
stories.author: Yiwei Lu
bullet_points: The research addresses a critical vulnerability in LLM post-training pipelines (SFT, RLHF, DPO) by modeling sequential data poisoning.
It introduces a novel threat model that accounts for the possibility of multiple attackers targeting different stages of the training process.
This work is highly relevant to AI safety and trustworthiness, as it examines the integrity of the entire LLM development lifecycle.

stories.title: Audio Interaction Model
stories.author: Chunyan Miao
bullet_points: Proposes the 'Audio Interaction Model' to unify current siloed audio models (offline LALMs, single-task streaming models).
The core concept is an 'always-on perceive-decide-respond loop' enabling real-time, dynamic interaction with sound and environment.
Introduces 'Audio-Interaction' as the realization of this unified, online LALM architecture.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
A key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
The framework includes built-in support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM usage.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for easily setting up and running coding tools.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex, both locally and in the cloud.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now offers experimental local image generation capabilities on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models entirely on their local machine.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local LLM tools and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a working directory.
Users can leverage various open-weight alternatives (e.g., gpt-oss:20b, gpt-oss:120b) instead of proprietary models.

stories.title: Nintendo confirms it will sell a new Switch 2 with replaceable battery in the EU
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Nintendo is planning to release the Switch 2 in the EU with a replaceable battery option.
This change is a direct response to upcoming EU regulations set to take effect in February 2027.
The move signals a shift towards greater hardware repairability in response to environmental and consumer legislation.

stories.title: Apple is bringing age verification to Texas this week
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Apple is implementing age verification in the Texas App Store.
The rollout is scheduled to begin on Thursday, June 4th.
This action follows a federal appeals court allowing Texas' App Store Accountability Act to take effect.

stories.title: The best Qi2 batteries for iPhone and Pixel
stories.author: Cameron Faulkner
bullet_points: Qi2.2-certified power banks are offering significant speed improvements for wireless charging.
These new batteries can charge compatible iPhones (like the iPhone 16 or later) at up to 25W wirelessly.
The integration of MagSafe magnetic snapping enhances the usability of these compact power banks.

stories.title: As AI gets better, it reveals an empty promise
stories.author: TC. Sottek
bullet_points: The article features a hands-on review of Google's new Gemini AI agent, Spark.
The AI agent demonstrates highly effective, almost uncanny, personalization capabilities.
The review suggests the AI's effectiveness is so advanced it is 'scary,' hinting at significant capability growth.

stories.title: PlayStation is getting back to what it’s good at
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: PlayStation is shifting its development focus away from live-service games.
The console manufacturer is prioritizing premium, narrative-driven, single-player titles.
This strategic pivot was highlighted during the recent State of Play showcase.

stories.title: Can't make sense of Dashlane's vault theft notification? You're not alone.
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: Dashlane has experienced a vault theft incident, prompting a security advisory.
The advisory is criticized for omitting crucial details regarding the breach.
Dashlane has maintained a policy of silence concerning the specifics of the theft.

stories.title: Autonomous vehicles were supposed to cut traffic—what if they don't?
stories.author: Jonathan M. Gitlin
bullet_points: Waymo's robotaxis are exhibiting low passenger utilization rates.
Data indicates that nearly half of the miles driven by these autonomous vehicles are empty.
The finding challenges the initial premise that autonomous vehicles will inherently solve traffic congestion.

stories.title: Inside Meta's attempts to play catch-up with AI
stories.author: Hannah Murphy, Financial Times
bullet_points: The article discusses Meta's ongoing efforts to improve its Artificial Intelligence capabilities.
It addresses the competitive landscape, specifically Meta's challenge in catching up to rivals in AI development.
The core theme revolves around lingering doubts regarding Meta's ability to close the technological gap.

stories.title: Imaginative Perception Tokens Enhance Spatial Reasoning in Multimodal Language Models
stories.author: Ranjay Krishna
bullet_points: The research addresses a known weakness in Vision Language Models (VLMs): poor spatial reasoning when information is occluded or unseen.
A novel mechanism, Imaginative Perception Tokens (IPT), is introduced to externalize and represent hypothetical perceptions.
IPTs allow VLMs to perform tasks requiring 'imaginative perception,' such as inferring views from unseen angles or tracing paths through hidden spaces.

stories.title: Language Models Need Sleep: Learning to Self-Modify and Consolidate Memories
stories.author: Vahab Mirrokni
bullet_points: Addresses a key limitation of current LLMs: the inability to continually learn and transfer temporal in-context knowledge to long-term parameters.
Proposes a mechanism inspired by human learning, suggesting LLMs require a 'sleep' or consolidation phase for effective memory management.
Focuses on enabling models to self-modify and consolidate memories, moving beyond purely instantaneous prediction capabilities.

stories.title: Quantifying Faithful Confidence Expression in Large Reasoning Models
stories.author: Arman Cohan
bullet_points: The paper addresses the critical issue of 'Faithful Confidence Expression' (FC) in Large Reasoning Models (LRMs).
FC refers to the alignment between a model's true uncertainty and the confidence it linguistically expresses to the user.
This research is important because users often interpret LRM reasoning traces as evidence of competence and confidence, making reliable uncertainty communication vital for trust.

stories.title: QUBRIC: Co-Designing Queries and Rubrics for RL Beyond Verifiable Rewards
stories.author: Chao Zhang
bullet_points: Introduces QUBRIC, a framework designed to advance Reinforcement Learning (RL) beyond reliance on simple, verifiable rewards.
Addresses a key bottleneck where rubric quality is limited by the structure of the queries used in RL training.
Proposes a method to co-design queries and rubrics simultaneously, overcoming issues where open-ended queries lead to vague rubrics.

stories.title: AlignAtt4LLM: Fast AlignAtt for Decoder-Only LLMs at IWSLT 2026 Simultaneous Speech Translation Task
stories.author: Dominik Macháček
bullet_points: Introduces AlignAtt4LLM, a new system for simultaneous speech translation (English to German, Italian, Chinese) for the IWSLT 2026 task.
The system utilizes a synchronous cascade, combining Qwen3-ASR with Gemma-4 E4B-it for translation.
It marks a novel application of AlignAtt to decoder-only LLMs, adapting the technique without traditional encoder-decoder cross-attention.

stories.title: Self-Refining Agentic Reinforcement Learning for Vision-Conditioned UAV Navigation
stories.author: Dzmitry Tsetserukou
bullet_points: Introduces AgenticRL, a framework designed to improve autonomy in reinforcement learning for robotics.
Addresses the limitations of traditional RL, specifically the heavy reliance on manual reward function design and fine-tuning.
Focuses on applying this self-refining learning to complex tasks like vision-conditioned navigation for UAVs (drones).

stories.title: NLLog: Lightweight, Explainable SOC Anomaly Detection via Log-to-Language Rewriting
stories.author: Daisuke Inoue
bullet_points: Introduces NLLog, a system for anomaly detection in security logs by rewriting structured logs into natural language sentences.
Utilizes TF-IDF weighting and tree ensembles for session classification, aiming to improve automated analysis.
Incorporates TreeSHAP to provide explainability, allowing security analysts to review the evidence behind detected anomalies.

stories.title: AdaKoop: Efficient Modeling of Nonlinear Dynamics from Nonstationary Data Streams with Koopman Operator Regression
stories.author: Yasushi Sakurai
bullet_points: Introduces 'AdaKoop,' a method for efficiently modeling nonlinear dynamics in nonstationary data streams.
Leverages Koopman operator theory to make complex nonlinear systems computationally tractable for real-time analysis.
Addresses the challenge of capturing dynamically changing nonlinear patterns under strict time constraints.

stories.title: Data Attribution in Large Language Models via Bidirectional Gradient Optimization
stories.author: Roger Wattenhofer
bullet_points: Addresses the critical, open problem of data provenance in Large Language Models (LLMs).
Proposes a Training Data Attribution (TDA) method for auto-regressive LLMs.
Uses an expanded inverse formulation to determine which training data influenced a model's output.

stories.title: Reproducing, Analyzing, and Detecting Reward Hacking in Rubric-Based Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Xiaozhi Wang
bullet_points: The paper addresses 'reward hacking' in Rubric-Based Reinforcement Learning (RL) where LLMs act as judges.
Reward hacking occurs when policy models exploit latent biases in the LLM-as-a-Judge (LaaJ) for ineffective training.
The authors introduce CHERRL, a controllable environment designed to analyze and mitigate these subtle hacking behaviors.

stories.title: STRIDE: Training Data Attribution via Sparse Recovery from Subset Perturbations
stories.author: Zhijing Jin
bullet_points: The research addresses Training Data Attribution (TDA), the challenge of linking LLM predictions back to their training data.
Traditional TDA methods using causal interventions are computationally infeasible for large models due to the need for repeated retraining.
The proposed method, STRIDE, offers a computationally lighter alternative by approximating data attribution using sparse recovery from subset perturbations.

stories.title: Beyond Text Following: Repairable Arbitration Reversals in Audio-Language Models
stories.author: Daling Wang
bullet_points: The research addresses a critical failure mode in Audio-Language Models (ALMs) where models prioritize conflicting text over clear audio evidence.
The study uses a 'same-audio counterfactual' method to isolate whether the audio evidence is ignored or suppressed by conflicting text inputs.
Results indicate that a significant portion (64.1%) of conflict samples show a measurable shift in model preference when the conflicting text is removed.

stories.title: Streaming Communication in Multi-Agent Reasoning
stories.author: Ying-Cong Chen
bullet_points: Addresses the latency bottleneck in multi-agent reasoning systems, which typically use a 'generate-then-transfer' paradigm.
Introduces StreamMA, a novel system that streams reasoning steps immediately to downstream agents for pipelining.
Demonstrates that this pipelining not only reduces latency but also surprisingly improves overall reasoning effectiveness.

stories.title: Failed Reasoning Traces Tell You What Is Fixable (But Not by Reading Them)
stories.author: Eilif B. Muller
bullet_points: The paper addresses the common practice of simply increasing compute when LLMs fail reasoning tasks.
It argues that failed inference traces contain a crucial signal about the nature of the failure (e.g., sampling error vs. structural flaw).
The proposed method uses these traces to encode a 'recoverability structure,' suggesting targeted interventions rather than brute-force resampling.

stories.title: Activation-Based Active Learning for In-Context Learning: Challenges and Insights
stories.author: Stuart E. Middleton
bullet_points: The paper investigates using transformer model activations to guide active learning for selecting in-context examples in Large Language Models (LLMs).
It addresses a gap in prior work by applying activation-based deep active learning, moving beyond previous selection methods.
The research provides a comprehensive analysis of MLP activation-based deep active learning methods in the context of in-context learning.

stories.title: Self-Evaluation Is Already There: Eliciting Latent Judge Calibration in Base LLMs with Minimal Data
stories.author: Zhenkai Liang
bullet_points: Base LLMs possess an inherent, latent ability to predict external judge scores without specific fine-tuning.
The research introduces 'Self-Evaluation Elicitation' (SEE) to surface this pre-existing calibration ability.
The method demonstrates that few-shot prompting is sufficient for base models to predict multi-attribute quality scores above chance.

stories.title: Continual Visual and Verbal Learning Through a Child's Egocentric Input
stories.author: Mengye Ren
bullet_points: Introduces BabyCL, a continual multimodal learning framework designed to mimic how children learn language.
Addresses a limitation in prior work where neural networks required cycling through shuffled data, unlike real-world chronological input.
Utilizes the SAYCam dataset to train models on continuous, egocentric visual and verbal streams.

stories.title: Evaluating Large Language Models in Dynamic Clinical Decision-Making with Standardized Patient Cases
stories.author: Weidi Xie
bullet_points: The paper addresses the limitation of static benchmarks for evaluating LLMs in real-world clinical settings.
It proposes using Standardized Patient (SP) cases, traditionally used in medical education, to test LLMs' dynamic decision-making capabilities.
The research focuses on assessing how LLMs handle longitudinal care, including information gathering, treatment planning, and adaptation across patient states.

stories.title: I built a vulnerable app and spent $1,500 seeing if LLMs could hack it
stories.author: jc4p
bullet_points: A developer intentionally created a vulnerable application.
The experiment tested the capability of Large Language Models (LLMs) to perform hacking/exploitation.
The project involved a financial investment of $1,500 to conduct the security test.

stories.title: Uber's $1,500/month AI limit is a useful signal for AI tool pricing
stories.author: pdyc
bullet_points: Uber is implementing usage caps (e.g., $1,500/month) on AI tools like Claude Code.
This strategy signals a shift in how large enterprises manage and budget for generative AI usage.
The move suggests that AI tool pricing and consumption limits are becoming a key operational consideration for major tech companies.

stories.title: Run AI Coding Agents Safely with Docker Sandboxes
stories.author: pradumnasaraf
bullet_points: The article addresses the security risks associated with running AI coding agents.
It proposes using Docker sandboxes as a method to safely execute these agents.
The core problem is preventing untrusted AI agents from executing harmful commands or modifying systems.

stories.title: 87% of My Context Was Garbage: How I Optimized Claude Code Token Usage
stories.author: quolu
bullet_points: The article addresses a common pain point: excessive token usage in large language models (LLMs) like Claude.
The author details practical optimization strategies to manage and reduce API/plan costs.
It provides a real-world case study on improving efficiency when working with context windows.
